articles.summary: Testing web apps can be brutal: a designer changes one CSS class, and your entire test suite explodes.

articles.title: See AI testing that actually work 👀
SVELTE, MARKDOWN, AND THE MAGIC OF WEB COMPONENTS
CONTENT ADDRESSABLE STORAGE
articles.summary: Content Addressable Storage (CAS) uses cryptographic hashes of file content for addressing, making sure that identical content always generates the same hash. The technique solves problems related to browser caching and data duplication. This article goes over a Rust implementation of a CAS system, starting with a simple in-memory store and progressing to a persistent store that optimizes file system layout for performance.

articles.title: IMMUTABLE BY DEFAULT: HOW TO AVOID HIDDEN STATE BUGS IN OOP
articles.summary: Immutable objects should be used by default in object-oriented programming to avoid hidden state bugs caused by unexpected data modifications. This post shows a DateTime example where a shared mutable object led to a difficult-to-trace bug. Immutability prevents such issues by making it so data cannot be changed after creation, whereas the performance overhead is minimal.

articles.title: TEAM OKRS IN ACTION
articles.summary: Team OKRs work best when teams define their own objectives collaboratively rather than having goals imposed from above, as top-down cascades often result in weak commitment and disengagement. High-performing teams bridge the gap between organizational strategy and execution by creating their own OKRs that align with broader company direction while maintaining ownership and autonomy.

articles.title: VIBE CODING TIPS AND TRICKS
articles.summary: Effective vibe coding requires clear requirements, design guidelines, strategic prompting, and proper testing with human-defined test cases. AI should be used to help co-author documentation, both for humans and AI to read later.

articles.title: SMARTER DEBUGGING WITH SENTRY MCP AND CURSOR: STEP-BY-STEP GUIDE
articles.summary: Debugging production issues with Cursor = constant context switching. Alt-tab to Sentry, copy error details, switch back to IDE, paste into chat, etc. Sentry's MCP server [10] gives Cursor direct access to your production error data, stack traces, and user impact metrics — so the AI has _all_ the context with no copy-and-pasting required. articles.title: BYTEBOT
articles.summary: Bytebot is an open-source AI desktop agent that gives AI its own virtual computer with full application access to automate tasks via natural language. It can complete complex workflows, process various document formats, and use real applications within a complete Ubuntu environment.

articles.title: REDWOODSDK
articles.summary: RedwoodSDK is a React framework for building server-side web applications on Cloudflare. It uses a Vite plugin for features like SSR, React Server Components, and real-time functionality. The framework has a standards-based router with middleware and interruptors for precise control over requests and responses.

articles.title: WHISPERING
articles.summary: Whispering is an open-source speech-to-text application designed to be local-first and transparent with user data. It lets users transcribe speech quickly using a shortcut and uses cloud or local providers without a middleman.

articles.title: SECURE BOOT, TPM, AND ANTI-CHEAT ENGINES
articles.summary: Online multiplayer games are increasingly requiring Secure Boot and TPM to combat cheating by using hardware and firmware security. Secure Boot verifies the boot process, while TPM provides a unique hardware identifier and verifiable boot state. These measures aim to prevent unauthorized kernel-level drivers and ban evasion by uniquely identifying cheaters' hardware.

articles.title: WHO INVENTED BACKPROPAGATION?
articles.summary: Efficient backpropagation (used in modern neural networks) was first published by Seppo Linnainmaa in 1970, who built on precursor work by Henry J. Kelley in 1960. The minimization of errors through gradient descent in neural networks using dynamic programming and the chain rule has been discussed since the early 1960s. Despite its early origins, it took four decades for backpropagation to gain widespread acceptance.

articles.title: AI AGENT DESIGN: HOW TO BUILD RELIABLE AI AGENT ARCHITECTURE
articles.summary: There are three non-negotiable principles for building reliable AI agents: modular and role-based design, deep observability from the start, and feedback loops for iterative optimization. Modular design breaks down complex tasks into smaller, specialized agents for easier testing and scaling, while deep observability involves tracking metrics to identify and address issues. Implementing feedback loops allows for continuous learning and improvement through user input, automated evaluations, and self-reflection.

articles.title: FRACTIONAL JOBS
articles.summary: Fractional Jobs is a platform that connects companies with expert part-time engineering talent for monthly retainer-based work.

articles.title: WHAT COULD HAVE BEEN
articles.summary: Instead of blindly chasing AI hype, tech companies should focus on improving existing software and infrastructure, which are being neglected in favor of AI features that often degrade user experience.

articles.title: YOUR MCP DOESN'T NEED 30 TOOLS: IT NEEDS CODE
articles.summary: You can replace complex, multi-tool Model Context Protocol (MCP) setups with a single "ubertool" MCP that accepts and executes code (like Python or JavaScript) within a stateful environment.

articles.title: SIMPLICITY, VANISHED?! SOLVING THE MYSTERY WITH INERTIA.JS + RAILS
articles.summary: Inertia.js with Rails can be used to build modern JavaScript components and interactive UIs without the complexity of SPAs.
